questions.question: Find the determinant of the matrix $\begin{pmatrix} 4 & 2 \\ 3 & 5 \end{pmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: For a 2×2 matrix $\begin{pmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{pmatrix}$, the determinant is $ad - bc$. Here: $4(5) - 2(3) = 20 - 6 = 14$.

questions.question: Multiply the matrices: $\begin{pmatrix} 2 & 1 \\ 0 & 3 \end{pmatrix} \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 4 \\ 2 & 1 \end{pmatrix}$. Enter the entry in row 1, column 2.
questions.explanation: Row 1 of first matrix times column 2 of second matrix: $(2)(4) + (1)(1) = 8 + 1 = 9$.

questions.question: If $A = \begin{pmatrix} 3 & -1 \\ 2 & 4 \end{pmatrix}$ and $B = \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 \end{pmatrix}$, what is $A + B$? Enter the entry in row 2, column 1.
A matrix has determinant 6. If two rows are swapped, what is the determinant of the resulting matrix?
questions.explanation: Swapping two rows of a matrix multiplies the determinant by −1. Original determinant 6 becomes $6 \times (-1) = -6$.

questions.question: Compute $3 \begin{pmatrix} 2 & -1 \\ 0 & 4 \end{pmatrix}$. What is the entry in row 1, column 2?
questions.explanation: Scalar multiplication distributes: $3 \begin{pmatrix} 2 & -1 \\ 0 & 4 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 6 & -3 \\ 0 & 12 \end{pmatrix}$. Row 1, column 2 is $-3$.
